headline: Red Rock Rave #1 underway in Las Vegas — 16s, 17s, 18s competing for GJNC bids
body: The SCVA Red Rock Rave Junior National Qualifier #1 is running March 14–16 at the Las Vegas Convention Center. Divisions in play: 18 Open, 17 Open, 16 Open, 16 USA, 16 Liberty, 16 American, 15 Open, 15 USA. This is a USAV-sanctioned event with bids to the Girls Junior National Championships on the line. College coaches from Big West, WCC, Pac-12, and major programs nationwide are on the floor evaluating the 2027, 2028, and 2029 classes. Results post to sportwrench.com as play concludes. Red Rock Rave #2 follows April 4–6 at Mandalay Bay Convention Center.

headline: House settlement reshapes D1 volleyball — 18-player roster caps replace 12-scholarship limit, NLI eliminated
body: The House v. NCAA settlement fundamentally changed college volleyball as of July 1, 2025. D1 eliminated sport-specific scholarship limits — women's volleyball programs can now offer scholarships to all 18 rostered players, up from 12. The 60-year-old National Letter of Intent was eliminated in October 2024; recruits now sign financial aid agreements. For 2029 recruits, programs may carry more players per class but every spot is more competitive. Ask coaches directly: are you offering from 12 or 18?

headline: D1 contact period open through July 31 — weekday quiet window in effect through April
body: The NCAA D1 contact period runs January 16 – July 31, 2026. Coaches can email, text, call, and DM recruits now. Key constraint: Monday–Thursday through early April, D1 coaches are in a weekday quiet window — evaluation at weekend events only. Every D1 coach at Red Rock Rave is concentrated on Saturday and Sunday play. For 2029 recruits, verbal offers open June 15 after sophomore year.

headline: USAV GJNCs 16–17s headed to Dallas June 30–July 3 — bid hunt is on
body: The 2026 USAV Girls Junior National Championships for 16–17s will be held June 30–July 3 at the Kay Bailey Hutchinson Convention Center in Dallas, TX. Teams must earn bids through national qualifiers or receive at-large bids from USAV after the qualifier season closes. Red Rock Rave #1 this weekend is the first major bid opportunity of 2026 for 16s. At-large applications require complete AES results submission.

headline: Red Rock Rave #2 set for April 4–6 at Mandalay Bay — younger divisions compete for bids
body: The SCVA Red Rock Rave JNQ #2 runs April 4–6 at Mandalay Bay Convention Center in Las Vegas. Divisions: 15 Liberty, 15 American, 14s, 13s, 12s, 11s. USAV-sanctioned. Bids to Girls Junior Nationals on the line for eligible age groups. Registration via SportWrench. Monitor trickle-down standings from older age qualifiers heading in.

headline: PrepVolleyball 2029 class: California clubs dominating the libero position nationally
body: PrepVolleyball's 2029 class rankings show SoCal clubs producing the deepest libero talent pool in the country. Multiple players are drawing D1 attention before their first high school season. Rankings update within days of major qualifier weekends — Red Rock Rave #1 results this weekend are expected to trigger significant movement in the top 100. Full rankings require a PrepVolleyball Premier subscription.

headline: Weekday quiet period means every D1 coach at Red Rock Rave is watching this weekend only
body: D1 coaches are in a weekday evaluation quiet period through early April — no evaluating at club practices Monday–Thursday. Weekend qualifier events like Red Rock Rave are fully open. This means coaches in Las Vegas this weekend are 100% focused on Saturday and Sunday play. Saturday pool play and Sunday bracket play are the highest-stakes evaluation moments of the spring. Performing at peak on weekends drives D1 interest in this window.
